title: Outpost application onboarding manifest (outpost.app.yaml)
description: Schema for outpost.app.yaml — the per-application descriptor that lives at the root of an APPLICATION repo (not the infras repo). Consumed by `outpost onboard <repo-or-path>` to render Caddy fragments, compose overrides, and/or k8s manifests into the local infras instance. Stable across patch and minor versions; new optional fields are additive.

apiVersion.description: Schema version. Locked to outpost.dev/v1 — bumped on breaking changes only.

kind.description: Resource kind. Always 'App' for this schema.

name.description: Short DNS-1123 label. Used as the Caddyfile.d/<name>.caddy basename, the compose service name, and the k8s Application name. Must be unique within an outpost instance.

description.description: One-line human description. Surfaced in `outpost status` and verify reports.

tier.description: Architectural tier. **Strict contract:** `k3s` is the default for ALL applications (anything stateless — HTTP servers, workers, queues consumers, ML inference, etc.). These get the `*.apps.<ROOT_DOMAIN>` wildcard ingress for free via k3s Traefik, no Cloudflare changes. `compose` is RESERVED for stateful infrastructure additions (extra databases, message queues, search engines, etc.) that live alongside Postgres/Redis/RabbitMQ/Manticore on the data layer — these go through Caddy on top-level subdomains and require manual Cloudflare Tunnel configuration. If you're not adding a stateful data service, choose `k3s`.

compose.description: Required when tier=compose. Defines the container the app runs as.

container_name.description: Container name on the infra network. Used by other services (and Caddy fragments) to address the app. Defaults to metadata.name.

ports.description: Optional list of ports to expose on the host (for direct localhost access during development). Format: 'HOST:CONTAINER' or 'CONTAINER' for ephemeral host port.

env.description: Environment variables to inject. Values may reference outpost vars like ${ROOT_DOMAIN}, ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} — resolved at compose render time.

env_from_outpost.description: Convenience list: names of outpost-managed vars (ROOT_DOMAIN, POSTGRES_URL, REDIS_URL, etc.) to expose to the container without explicit env entries.

volumes.description: Optional volume mounts. Standard docker-compose volume syntax.

depends_on.description: Optional list of infra services this app depends on (postgres, redis, rabbitmq, manticore).

k3s.description: Required when tier=k3s. Points at the manifest repo + image registry that drive the GitOps deploy.

manifest_repo.description: Optional override for the manifests repo URL. Falls back to MANIFEST_REPO_URL from .env. Supports envsubst syntax — e.g. ${MY_APP_MANIFEST_REPO:-https://default/...}.

manifest_branch.description: Optional override for the deploy branch. Defaults to OUTPOST_DEPLOY_BRANCH from .env.

manifest_path.description: Optional override for the path inside the manifest repo to sync. Defaults to apps/<metadata.name>.

routes.description: **tier=compose ONLY.** Declarative Caddy route specification for stateful infrastructure additions. Rendered to Caddyfile.d/<name>.caddy at onboard time. Each entry is one host block; rules within a host evaluate in order. For routing patterns the declarative form cannot express, use spec.caddy_fragment. **Forbidden for tier=k3s** — applications expose themselves via Kubernetes IngressRoute in the manifest repo, not via Caddy fragments. `outpost onboard` will reject tier=k3s + routes.

host.description: Host header to match. **Must be a top-level subdomain** of the form `<prefix>.${ROOT_DOMAIN}` (e.g. `search.example.com`). May embed Caddy env-var syntax: `{$MY_HOST:myhost}.{$ROOT_DOMAIN}`. **Hosts containing `.apps.` are rejected** — that wildcard belongs to the k3s ingress; stateful infra must live at the top level (and requires a matching Cloudflare Tunnel Public Hostname).

rules.description: Path-based sub-routes inside this host. Evaluated in order.

path.description: List of path matchers (e.g. ['/api/v1', '/api/v1/']). May use wildcards: '/api/*'.

rewrite.description: Full path rewrite — replaces the matched URI with this string before proxying.

rewrite_path_prefix.description: Two-element [old_prefix, new_prefix]. Strips old_prefix from the URI and prepends new_prefix before proxying. Mutually exclusive with rewrite.

upstream.description: Backend target: 'container:port'. Container must be on the infra network (or use host.docker.internal:<port> for host-network services).

default_upstream.description: Catch-all upstream for any request to this host that didn't match a rule. Optional — without it, unmatched paths return 404.

caddy_fragment.description: **tier=compose ONLY.** Raw Caddy DSL escape hatch for stateful infrastructure additions. When `routes` cannot express a needed pattern (complex matchers, custom directives, header manipulation), provide the full fragment as a string. Mutually exclusive with `routes` — if both are set, onboard fails. The fragment must NOT declare its own `:80 { ... }` site block; it is inserted inside the main site block of the infras Caddyfile. **Forbidden for tier=k3s** (same rationale as `routes`).

oneOf.description: Either declarative routes or a raw caddy_fragment, never both.
